Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Litigation Pending Completion Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Fulvio Cajina, ESq., Telephone 415-601-0779, appearing for Plaintiff Patricia Kubichek;
Mary Shea, shea Law Offices, Telephone 510-208-4422, appearing for Plaintiff Patricia Kubichek;
Hayden R. Pace, Esq.,Stokes Wagner, Telephone 404-766-0076, appearing for Defendants Kimpton Sir Francisco Drake Hotel, et al

RULING - ARGUED: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Kimpton Sir Francis Drake Hotel, Kimpton Hotel & Restaurant Group, LLC, and KHRG Employer, LLC's motion to stay litigation pending completion of arbitration is granted. In the interest of judicial economy and to avoid possibly inconsistent results, Ms. Kubichek's claims that are not subject to arbitration are stayed pending the resolution of the arbitration proceedings. Prevailing party to prepare and submit form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Fulvio Cajina, ESq., telephone 415-601-0779, appearing for Plaintiff Patricia Kubichek;
Mary Shea, shea Law Offices, Telephone 510-208-4422, appearing for Plaintiff Patricia Kubichek;
Hayden R. Pace, Esq.,Stokes Wagner, Telephone 404-766-0076, appearing for Defendants Kimpton Sir Francisco Drake Hotel, et al

RULING - ARGUED: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Kimpton Sir Francis Drake Hotel, Kimpton Hotel & Restaurant Group, LLC, and KHRG Employer, LLC's motion to compel arbitration is granted. Plaintiff Patricia Kubichek entered into an arbitration agreement with defendants and the claims Ms. Kubichek alleges in her complaint are "employment-related disputes" as that phrase is defined in the parties' arbitration agreement. Ms. Kubichek's arguments based on her limited proficiency in English lack merit. (Ramos v. Westlake Services LLC (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 674, 687 ("Under the general contract principles ' the fact that [plaintiff] signed a contract in a language he may not have completely understood would not bar enforcement of the arbitration agreement. If [plaintiff] did not speak or understand English sufficiently to comprehend the English Contract, he should have had it read or explained to him.")). The agreement's language that the parties agree to submit to binding arbitration any employment related disputes "which are not resolved though the Property's employee appeal procedure or another informal means" is fairly construed to provide that all unresolved employment related disputes must be submitted to arbitration. This construction is consistent with the provision that an employee is not required to go through preliminary steps of dispute resolution before submitting a dispute to arbitration and that the parties have waived their right to a jury trial. Even if Ms. Kubichek's interpretation was adopted and the agreement's language was construed to mean that as a condition precedent to the employee's duty to arbitrate the employer is obliged to engage in an informal dispute resolution, there is no evidence that Ms. Kubicheck timely sought to appeal her termination through the employee appeal procedure or to resolve her claims through other informal means or that her employer refused to engage in such dispute resolution mechanisms. Assuming without deciding that the arbitration agreement had a high degree of procedural unconscionability, the agreement is enforceable because Ms. Kubichek has failed to show that the agreement contains any substantive unconscionable provisions. (Pinnacle Museum Tower Assn. v. Pinnacle Market Development (US), LLC (2012) 55 Cal.4th 223, 247 ("Both procedural unconscionability and substantive unconscionability must be shown, but 'they need not be present in the same degree' and are evaluated on 'a sliding scale.'") Prevailing party to prepare and submit form of order pursuant to CRC 3.1312
